// Fixture for TestNoGracefulNilInRequestPaths.
//
// This file is .go.txt (not .go) so `go test` and `go build` don't compile
// it. The walker parses it from disk as raw source.
//
// ILLEGAL: a daemon RPC handler silently returns success when its
// dependency is nil. This is the one-code-path anti-pattern the test
// exists to prevent. The walker MUST flag it.

package daemon

import "context"

type fakeAuthz struct{}
type fakePool struct{}

type illegalServer struct {
	authz *fakeAuthz
	pool  *fakePool
}

// RPC handler should always run. A nil dep must fail startup, never
// silently skip at request time.
func (s *illegalServer) Handle(ctx context.Context) error {
	if s.authz == nil {
		// ANTI-PATTERN: silent skip. Walker must flag THIS line.
		return nil
	}
	return nil
}

// Same anti-pattern in a different return shape.
func (s *illegalServer) HandleTuple(ctx context.Context) (bool, error) {
	if s.pool == nil {
		// ANTI-PATTERN: silent skip. Walker must flag THIS line too.
		return true, nil
	}
	return true, nil
}
